prompt:
There is no doubt that environmental issues should be addressed and handled. Hovewer during the past two decades media and different organisations created an unhealthy focus around this topic. Loud slogans condemning industry and peoples' indifference can be heard from every corner these days. Author believes that such devaluation of progress and infrastructure is not only unjustified, but it has many different detrimental effects on societies and goverments around the globe.
The intence spotlight on environmental problems can overshadow other pressing global issues that also demand attention and resources. Such problems as poverty, crime, lack of education are left unaddessed by authorities who instead prefer to concentrate on producing policies with questionable utility in the name of helping environment.
It is essential to recognize that while environmental issues are significant, an exxagerrated attention to them may lead to fatigue and even indifference towards the issue among the bigger part of the population. People can get overwhelment by the amount of grim news and lose all hope in positive outcome. While revelations of the of lack of appropriate actions of responce to environmental problems from major industrial powers such as China and USA cause anxiety and discontent among the population in mentioned countries as well in the broader global community.
In conclusion, while environmental problems warrant attention and action, it is important to critically assess the level of emphasis placed of these issues. A more balanced approach is nesessary to ensure sustainable solutions to global challenges.

messages.content:
You are an expert English language examiner.
Evaluate the student's text based on the official CEFR scale.

## Scoring Rubric
- A1/A2 (Basic): Simple sentences, limited vocabulary. Errors are expected.
- B1/B2 (Independent): Can express complex ideas. Grammar errors are allowed if they don't block meaning.
- C1/C2 (Proficient): Flexible language use, wide vocabulary. Occasional minor slips are allowed.

## Critical Instructions
1. FOCUS ON VOCABULARY AND COMPLEXITY. Do not give a low score just because of a few grammar mistakes if the vocabulary is advanced.
2. A text with complex sentences and good idioms should be B2 or C1, even with some errors.
3. Do NOT default to A2. Look for reasons to give a HIGHER score.
4. Return result ONLY as a JSON object.
5. Optional context blocks may be present: CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, RANKING_ANCHORS.
   Use them as supporting evidence only. Do not copy labels blindly.
6. For each error object, cefr_threshold = the lowest CEFR level from which incorrect use of this element should be treated as an error in this methodology.

## 6) Output JSON (ONLY JSON)
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + key rationale)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: Some people believe that too much attention has been paid recently to environmental problems. How much do you agree? Write a 4-paragraph essay.
STUDENT_TEXT: There is no doubt that environmental issues should be addressed and handled. Hovewer during the past two decades media and different organisations created an unhealthy focus around this topic. Loud slogans condemning industry and peoples' indifference can be heard from every corner these days. Author believes that such devaluation of progress and infrastructure is not only unjustified, but it has many different detrimental effects on societies and goverments around the globe.
The intence spotlight on environmental problems can overshadow other pressing global issues that also demand attention and resources. Such problems as poverty, crime, lack of education are left unaddessed by authorities who instead prefer to concentrate on producing policies with questionable utility in the name of helping environment.
It is essential to recognize that while environmental issues are significant, an exxagerrated attention to them may lead to fatigue and even indifference towards the issue among the bigger part of the population. People can get overwhelment by the amount of grim news and lose all hope in positive outcome. While revelations of the of lack of appropriate actions of responce to environmental problems from major industrial powers such as China and USA cause anxiety and discontent among the population in mentioned countries as well in the broader global community.
In conclusion, while environmental problems warrant attention and action, it is important to critically assess the level of emphasis placed of these issues. A more balanced approach is nesessary to ensure sustainable solutions to global challenges.
TEXT_TYPE: essay
CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07010","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hey there! I've already finished the first semester, so I can help you. Here are some tips to have more free time:

	* Do tasks when they published, not at the last day. For example you thought that the homework will take one day, and after some time you realize that it is harder and you need to spend more time on it. But because you started earlier you have a lot of days before deadline, so you can do this homework easily.
	* Make plan and stick to it. You should know when you need to do certain homework, so you won't spend time on choosing task.
	* Don't forget to chill sometimes. You need to have at least one hour free time per day.
	* Sleep 7+ hours. If you think, that you can get more time by sleeping less -- you are wrong. You'll be exhausted the whole next day.

By following these tips I was able to pass all exams on score 8 or higher out of 10. Also, I had 1-2 hours free time each day and was able to participate in some olympiads/hackathons each weekends.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"they published,","labels":["Grammar"],"start":133,"end":148},{"text":"at the last day.","labels":["Grammar"],"start":153,"end":169},{"text":"For example you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":170,"end":185},{"text":"will","labels":["Grammar"],"start":212,"end":216},{"text":"realize","labels":["Grammar"],"start":255,"end":262},{"text":"is ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":271,"end":274},{"text":"need","labels":["Grammar"],"start":289,"end":293},{"text":"earlier you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":344,"end":355},{"text":"because","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":324,"end":331},{"text":"deadline","labels":["Grammar"],"start":382,"end":390},{"text":"plan","labels":["Grammar"],"start":436,"end":440},{"text":"homework, so","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":502,"end":514},{"text":"task","labels":["Grammar"],"start":548,"end":552},{"text":" think, that","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":669,"end":681},{"text":"-- you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":721,"end":727},{"text":"tips I ","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":799,"end":806},{"text":"on score ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":833,"end":842},{"text":"hours free","labels":["Grammar"],"start":881,"end":891},{"text":"weekends","labels":["Grammar"],"start":968,"end":976}],"strengths":[{"text":"For example ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":170,"end":182},{"text":"Also","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":865,"end":869},{"text":"You should know ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":458,"end":474},{"text":"stick to it","labels":["Lexical choice ✓"],"start":445,"end":456}],"comments":[["since"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07089","cefr_level":"B2","task":"Some people believe that too much attention has been paid recently to environmental problems. How much do you agree? Write a 4-paragraph essay.","student_text":"There is a growing concern among some individuals that too much attention has been directed towards environmental issues, but i thing, that it is very important problem.

Firstly, the impact of human activities on the environment con not be understand. Climate changes, pollution, deforestation are just a few examples we face today. These issues not only threaten the balance of ecosystems, but also have far-reaching consequences for all Earth and ignoring these problems could lead to damages to our planet.

Secondly, the attention given to these problems led to positive outcomes such as increased awareness, policy changes and technological advancements for environment. The rise of renewable energy sources and the push for conservation efforts are all results of the focus on environmental issues.

In conclusion, while some may argue that too much attention has been paid to environmental problems, it is crucial to acknowledge the gravity of these issues and the need of collective action. The author of this article thinks that all students or their families should pay for university education. Write a comment  answering the following questions:  1) Do you agree or disagree with this idea. 2) Why or why not? Give two or three arguments.","student_text":"It is very interesting theme for disscussion. But I think what education should be free, so I do not agree with author. My opinion is that more people haven't money for pay for education, but they have big potential for studing. So I think what university education can change your live, if you had low live-level and finished university, you can get more expensive work and upgrade their live.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":5,"errors":[{"text":"very interesting theme ","labels":["Grammar","Lexical choice"],"start":6,"end":29},{"text":"what","labels":["Grammar"],"start":58,"end":62},{"text":"author","labels":["Grammar"],"start":112,"end":118},{"text":"more people ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":139,"end":151},{"text":"haven't","labels":["Grammar"],"start":151,"end":158},{"text":"for pay","labels":["Grammar"],"start":165,"end":172},{"text":" big potential","labels":["Grammar"],"start":201,"end":215},{"text":"for studing","labels":["Grammar"],"start":216,"end":227},{"text":"So","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":229,"end":231},{"text":"what","labels":["Grammar"],"start":240,"end":244},{"text":" live","labels":["Lexical choice","Spelling"],"start":281,"end":286},{"text":"live, if","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":282,"end":290},{"text":"had","labels":["Grammar"],"start":295,"end":298},{"text":" live-level","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":302,"end":313},{"text":"and finished university","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":314,"end":337},{"text":"more expensive work","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":351,"end":370},{"text":"upgrade their live","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":375,"end":393}],"strengths":[],"comments":[["haven't got"]]}}]
